hero.title: Build
hero.description: Authentication, i18n, role-based access, CRUD modules, and a dashboard — all wired up and ready to extend.

overview.title: A full-stack template for real apps
overview.description: Not a component library. Not a starter kit full of TODOs. A working application — with auth, data, roles, and UI — ready to fork and extend.

itemCentric.label: Module-first
itemCentric.desc: Three CRUD modules (Items, Categories, Users) with real in-memory mock data. Add your own in minutes.

fullyAuditable.label: Role-based access
fullyAuditable.desc: Three roles (admin, editor, viewer) protect every page and action. Swap in your own authorization logic.

scanDriven.label: i18n built-in
scanDriven.desc: English and Spanish out of the box. next-intl with per-page namespaces and automatic locale routing.

sameDayBeyond.label: Dashboard included
sameDayBeyond.desc: An operational dashboard with stat cards, charts, and activity feeds — all connected to the live mock data.

interfaces.title: Two ways to
interfaces.description: The template ships with two user contexts built on the same codebase. Management users own the data; read-only users explore it.

management.title: Management
management.subtitle: Full control.
management.description: Admin and Editor users access every module, create and edit records, manage user accounts, and view the full dashboard.

readonly.title: Read-only
readonly.subtitle: Browse and view.
readonly.description: Viewer users can explore the full item catalog, see categories, and read the dashboard — but cannot modify any data.

flow.title: Item lifecycle — three stages.
flow.note: Status transitions are validated server-side. Reactivation from archived requires admin or editor access.

create.title: Create
create.summary: A new item is registered with a name, optional description, category, and images.

bullets.0: Fill in name and optional description
bullets.3: Item is saved as Active by default

manage.title: Manage
manage.summary: Items can be edited, recategorized, or deactivated at any time by users with editor or admin role.

bullets.3: Filter and search the full catalog

archive.title: Archive
archive.summary: Archived items are retained in the system for historical reference but removed from the active catalog.

principles.title: Design choices
principles.description: Four decisions that shape how the template is built — and how you should extend it.

principle1.title: In-memory first, database-ready
principle1.body: All data lives in a shared in-memory store on the server. Replace the store calls with Prisma, Drizzle, or any ORM — no other files need to change.

principle2.title: Server actions, not API routes
principle2.body: Every mutation goes through a typed Next.js Server Action. No REST boilerplate to maintain — just call the action from your form or hook.

principle3.title: Namespace-scoped translations
principle3.body: Each page and module loads only its own translation keys. Adding a new language means adding one JSON file — no code changes.

principle4.title: Roles enforced at the layout level
principle4.body: Access control lives in a single middleware and per-layout guard. Changing a role's permissions is a one-line edit in the route definition.

lifecycle.title: Status vocabulary
lifecycle.description: Items and users each carry a small set of well-defined statuses. Everything in the UI derives from these values.
lifecycle.txDescription: Every action in the app is gated by role. Roles are assigned at invite time and can be updated by an admin.

active.label: Active
active.desc: The item is visible in the catalog and fully operational.

inactive.label: Inactive
inactive.desc: The item is hidden from the default view but retained in the system.

archived.label: Archived
archived.desc: The item is closed and kept for historical reference only.

admin.label: Admin
admin.desc: Full access. Can create, edit, delete items and categories, manage users, and view the dashboard.

editor.label: Editor
editor.desc: Can create and edit items and categories. Cannot manage users or delete records.

viewer.label: Viewer
viewer.desc: Read-only. Can browse items and view the dashboard but cannot modify any data.

catalog.title: Demo data
catalog.description: The template ships with 20 pre-seeded items across these categories. All editable in the Items and Categories modules.

clothing.name: Clothing
clothing.desc: Apparel and footwear of all kinds.
clothing.examples: Winter jacket, running shoes, formal suit

accessories.name: Accessories
accessories.desc: Bags, watches, jewelry, and personal items.

sports.name: Sports
sports.desc: Sports gear, outdoor, and fitness items.
sports.examples: Mountain bike, yoga mat, climbing harness

other.name: Other
other.desc: Anything that doesn't fit the above.

roles.title: Who can do what
roles.description: Three roles ship with the template. Each role has a clear, non-overlapping set of permissions enforced at every layer.

admin.name: Admin
admin.summary: Full system access. Manages everything including users.

editor.name: Editor
editor.summary: Content management. Creates and edits data but cannot manage users.

viewer.name: Viewer
viewer.summary: Read-only access. Browses the catalog and monitors the dashboard.

closing.title: Four things to
closing.description: The template works out of the box. These four steps will make it yours.
closing.footerNote: All changes are isolated in well-marked files. The template is designed to be forked, not fought.

tip1.title: Replace the mock store
tip1.body: Swap lib/mock/in-memory-store.ts with your database adapter. All service functions call the same interface — no other files need to change.

tip2.title: Configure authentication
tip2.body: Auth.js is pre-configured with a Credentials provider. Add OAuth providers, a database session store, or a custom JWT strategy in auth.config.ts.

tip3.title: Update metadata and branding
tip3.body: Replace 'App Name' in messages/en.json and messages/es.json. Update the logo in public/. Change the primary color in tailwind.config.ts.

tip4.title: Add your own modules
tip4.body: Copy the modules/items directory as a starting point. Define your types, write a service, add Server Actions, and drop the module into the sidebar routes.

footer.description: A production-ready Next.js starter template with auth, i18n, CRUD modules, and dashboard.
footer.copyright: © {year} App Name. Open source under the MIT License.
footer.tagline: Fork it. Extend it. Ship it.
